# Mad Hatter Chimney Sweep — Full Business Profile

## Business Overview
Mad Hatter Chimney Sweep is a licensed, bonded, and insured chimney service company serving the Greater Seattle area since 1979. With over 2,500 satisfied customers and a 5-star rating, we are one of the most trusted chimney service providers in Washington State.

## Contact Information
- Phone: (206) 274-6409
- Email: services@themadhatterchimneysweep.com
- Website: https://www.themadhatterchimneysweep.com
- Address: Bothell, WA 98011
- Washington State Contractor License: MADHAHL790LW

## Business Hours
- Monday–Friday: 8:00 AM – 6:00 PM
- Saturday: 8:00 AM – 4:00 PM
- Sunday: Closed

## Payment
Accepted: Cash, Check, Credit Card (USD)
Price range: $$

---

## Services

### Chimney Sweep & Cleaning
Professional chimney cleaning and creosote removal. Removes soot, debris, and hazardous creosote deposits from fireplace flues. Annual cleaning is recommended by the CSIA for regularly used chimneys. Reduces chimney fire risk significantly.

### Chimney Inspection
Comprehensive chimney inspections following CSIA standards:
- Level 1: Visual inspection included with every sweep
- Level 2: Camera inspection for real estate transactions or after events
- Level 3: Invasive inspection when serious hazards are suspected

### Chimney Repair
Masonry repair, crown repair, tuckpointing, and structural restoration. Addresses cracked or spalled bricks, deteriorated mortar joints, damaged chimney crowns, and flashing issues.

### Chimney Cap Installation
Installation and replacement of chimney caps to protect flue openings from rain, animals, and debris. Prevents water damage, nesting, and draft issues.

### Chimney Liner Installation
Stainless steel and cast-in-place liner installation. Liners improve draft, contain combustion gases, and protect masonry from heat and acidic byproducts.

### Chimney Waterproofing
Professional application of penetrating waterproofing sealants to protect masonry from moisture intrusion, freeze-thaw damage, and deterioration common in the Pacific Northwest climate.

### Fireplace Cleaning
Complete fireplace cleaning and maintenance including firebox cleaning, damper inspection, and ash removal.

### Masonry Repair
Brick and mortar restoration, tuckpointing, and rebuilding for chimneys and exterior masonry structures.

### Dryer Vent Cleaning
Removal of lint buildup from dryer vent systems, improving appliance efficiency and reducing fire hazard.

---

## Service Area

Mad Hatter Chimney Sweep serves the Greater Seattle metropolitan area including:

**King County:** Seattle, Bellevue, Renton, Kent, Sammamish, Issaquah, Mercer Island, Newcastle, Kirkland, Redmond, Lake Forest Park, Shoreline

**Snohomish County:** Bothell, Woodinville, Kenmore, Edmonds, Lynnwood, Marysville, Everett, Snohomish, Monroe, Mill Creek, Mountlake Terrace, Mukilteo

---

## Frequently Asked Questions

**Q: How much does a chimney sweep cost in Seattle?**
A: A standard chimney sweep and inspection in Seattle typically costs $150–$250. Mad Hatter Chimney Sweep offers upfront pricing with no hidden fees. Call (206) 274-6409 for a free estimate.

**Q: How often should I get my chimney cleaned?**
A: The CSIA recommends annual chimney inspections and cleaning for any chimney that is used regularly. If you burn wood frequently, cleaning may be needed more often.

**Q: Is Mad Hatter Chimney Sweep licensed in Washington State?**
A: Yes. Mad Hatter Chimney Sweep holds WA Contractor License MADHAHL790LW and is fully licensed, bonded, and insured in Washington State.

**Q: What areas does Mad Hatter Chimney Sweep serve?**
A: We serve the Greater Seattle area including Bothell, Kirkland, Redmond, Bellevue, Everett, Woodinville, Kenmore, Shoreline, Edmonds, Lynnwood, and Marysville.

**Q: Do you offer chimney inspections?**
A: Yes. We perform CSIA-standard Level 1, Level 2, and Level 3 chimney inspections. A Level 1 inspection is included with every sweep.

**Q: What is a chimney cap and do I need one?**
A: A chimney cap covers the top of your flue to keep out rain, animals, and debris. Most homes need one and it can prevent costly water damage. We install and replace chimney caps.

**Q: Can a dirty chimney cause a house fire?**
A: Yes. Creosote buildup inside the flue is highly flammable and is the leading cause of chimney fires. Annual cleaning removes creosote and dramatically reduces fire risk.

---

## About the Company

Mad Hatter Chimney Sweep has been serving Seattle-area homeowners since 1979. Our technicians are trained to CSIA standards and provide thorough, professional service. We take pride in transparent pricing, reliable scheduling, and quality workmanship. Over 2,500 satisfied customers and hundreds of five-star reviews reflect our commitment to excellence.
